name: machine-sdcard
description: SD card interface over SPI bus. Mount as filesystem for expanded storage (logging, config files, data).
differences_from_cpython: Does NOT exist in CPython. MicroPython-specific SD card driver.

classes.name: SDCard
classes.signature: machine.SDCard(slot=1, width=1, cd=None, wp=None, sck=None, miso=None, mosi=None, cs=None, freq=20000000)

constructor_params.name: slot
constructor_params.description: SPI slot. 1 (HSPI) or 2 (VSPI, default) on ESP32.

constructor_params.name: cs
constructor_params.description: Chip select pin. Required for SD card.

constructor_params.name: freq
constructor_params.description: SPI frequency in Hz. Default 20MHz. Reduce to 1-5MHz if unreliable.

returns.description: Card information: capacity, sector size, etc.

methods.name: SDCard.ioctl
methods.description: Low-level IO control. Used by filesystem driver.

classes.example:
from machine import Pin, SDCard
import os

# SD card on VSPI (slot 2) with CS on GPIO 5
sd = SDCard(slot=2, cs=Pin(5), freq=20000000)

# Mount filesystem
os.mount(sd, '/sd')

# Use it
print(os.listdir('/sd'))

# Write a file
with open('/sd/data.txt', 'w') as f:
    f.write('Hello from ESP32!')

# Unmount before removal
os.umount('/sd')

esp32_notes: Requires external SD card module connected via SPI.
Mount at '/sd' (or any other path) for filesystem access.
VSPI (slot=2) default pins: sck=18, mosi=23, miso=19.
HSPI (slot=1) default pins: sck=14, mosi=13, miso=12.
Reduce freq to 1-5MHz if using long wires or breadboard.
Only one SD card can be mounted at a time.
Format SD card as FAT32 (not exFAT) for ESP32 compatibility.
